name: Jack Schlossberg
subject: Trump is already talking….
body:
Peter, I just got the most glowing endorsement...
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1]
Jack Schlossberg here, letting you know that our campaign in New York's 12th District has reached the attention of the White House.
Here's how I see it: If we're making MAGA mad, we're doing something right.
Before we unpack this, if you want to get in on our 'ridiculous' campaign, these first 48 hours are what set the tone for the race, so chip in if you can! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jack Schlossberg and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1&amount=25]
Earlier this week on MSNBC, I said that this admin is the most corrupt in our history – and I meant it. The disinformation that my cousin RFK Jr. is spreading is having real, deadly consequences. It doesn't take a genius to see he isn't making anybody healthier or safer.
No matter how much Karoline Leavitt wants people to believe that my cousin is "doing a phenomenal job bringing transparency and the gold standard of science back to our healthcare system," it doesn't make it true.
We need leaders who can face this fight head-on and call out corruption and lies when we see them.
I am ready to use my voice to stand up for truth, progress, and all of us. And I need you to have my back.
If the attacks are already coming from the top, it means we're on the right track. But we have our work cut out for us... Can I count on you to chip in? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jack Schlossberg and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112425_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112425_1&amount=25]
We're building a movement powered by people who know we deserve better, and I'm so glad to have you on the team. Let's keep it up !!
–Jack

Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. name: TN House Democratic Caucus
subject: Election 2026 Starts Now! TN House Democrats Pre-Session Fundraiser
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
The 2026 legislative session is approaching fast. It is a critical election year for Tennessee House Democrats, so let's kick it off together!
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KC9MFB6AB8G72Q1WJQYW6DV6_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KC9MFB6AB8G72Q1WJQYW6DV6_1)
---
| | | | Dear Peter, The 2026 legislative session is approaching fast. It is a critical election year for Tennessee House Democrats, so let's kick it off together!

Join us for our **Pre-Session Fundraiser **on ** Tuesday, January 7th** at Germantown Café in Nashville. This is a great opportunity to connect with Democratic lawmakers and network with fellow supporters, and it’s your last chance to donate before the legislative session reconvenes.

**Tuesday, January 7, 2026

** **5:30 PM – 7:00 PM

** **Germantown Café** at 1200 5th Ave N, Nashville, TN 37208 Our work in 2025 created momentum for a year of opportunity in 2026. First-quarter fundraising totals set the tone for everything that follows. With key races on the horizon, your support makes all the difference, because every dollar raised will be directly invested in winning races. Join us for a fun evening of good company and great conversation with friends who share your values. We hope to see you there!
---
| | [ RSVP NOW ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KC9MFB6AB8G72Q1WJQYW6DV6_2)
---
| Questions? Contact Abby at abby@triumphstrategies.com or 317-319-0015.
2026 will be a year to remember. With gratitude, **Tennessee House Democratic Caucus**
---
| | | |
---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
---

TN House Democratic Caucus PO Box 198082 Nashville, TN 37219

body:
Your commitment today strengthens everything we're fighting for.
| | |

| Peter, I'm reaching out personally because this campaign is bigger than politics – it's about the future of our communities, our families, and our state. _**And I can 't win this fight without you.**_ If we're going to defeat the political insiders and restore strong, common-sense leadership, we need a grassroots army of supporters who are willing to step up and take action. **That starts with volunteers – people like you who believe in our mission and are ready to help us reach voters across Virginia.** Whether you can knock doors, make phone calls, help at events, or volunteer from home, your involvement will make a real difference. [** _Will you join my volunteer team? >>>_**](https://xbdizvxp.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fbrycereeves.com%2F/2/0100019b134c70ed-77d730c5-f9cb-445a-ba08-0f29c76ccb7e-000000/IjVaIRyM7E7EOB3hrI76Bkz_Nsc=456)

name: Team Demand Justice PAC
subject: The Supreme Court gave Trump their blessing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251212-60985&t=1001&akid=60985%2E1407376%2EQ_KOgL ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251212-60985&t=1002&akid=60985%2E1407376%2EQ_KOgL ]Demand Justice PAC

The Supreme Court made a catastrophic mistake — and everyone is paying the
price. Last year, in Trump v. United States, the Court handed Donald Trump
absolute power, declaring that a president can essentially act above the
law.

Let that sink in: a 34-count convicted felon with dozens of alleged crimes
now has the Court’s blessing to ignore the rules that bind the rest of us.

And what has he done with it? Exactly what we feared.

Pardons for cronies who broke the law on his behalf. Attacks on political
opponents. Denials of disaster relief based on partisan politics.
Corruption disguised as “official acts.” Trump is weaponizing the
presidency to reward himself, his allies, and his billionaire buddies —
and the Supreme Court handed him the tools to do it.

This is not bold leadership. This is lawlessness. It’s tyranny in plain
sight. Justice Sotomayor warned this ruling would be “a loaded weapon” for
any president willing to put personal gain above the nation. She was
right. And every day, Americans see the consequences.

The Supreme Court has failed its most basic duty: protecting the
Constitution. It’s time to fix this mistake. No one — not a president, not
a billionaire, not a political operative — is above the law.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251212.-jcFbM&refcode=em20251212-60985&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=60985%2E1407376%2EQ_KOgL ]Demand Justice is fighting to hold the courts and lawmakers
accountable, but we need your voice. Stand with us by chipping in
$5 and help push for a justice system that protects
democracy, not undermines it.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251212.-jcFbM&amount=5&refcode=em20251212-60985&t=1022&akid=60985%2E1407376%2EQ_KOgL ]Donate $5 ››

Thank you,

Team Demand Justice PAC

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.demandjustice.org/survey/less-emails?t=1023&akid=60985%2E1407376%2EQ_KOgL

Email us: feedback@demandjustice.org

611 Pennsylvania Ave SE, #192, Washington DC 20003
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Ken Mitchell
subject: Reflections from the campaign trail
body:
Some of my thoughts on recent news | | | [](https://v8k6.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=693c3c8a3fb155167f0d33d5&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.kenmitchellforvirginia.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D693b386b16a7aa185b88b11f%26ss_email_id%3D693c3c8a3fb155167f0d33d5%26ss_campaign_name%3DReflections%2Bfrom%2Bthe%2Bcampaign%2Btrail%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-12-12T16%253A08%253A57Z&w=6578fd556ef54c70acb54115&c=b_693b386b16a7aa185b88b11f&l=en-US&s=BH-nV7AxbU7Xi-iIfmABkjQuiU0%3D)
---
|
#### **Friends-**
The past few weeks have been a whirlwind of developments that will impact all of us in the 6th district and across the country. A few keep coming to mind, and I’d like the opportunity to share my thoughts with you:
1. Trump’s plan to bail out farmers. Anyone who’s heard me talk about the importance of the 6th district has heard me talk about our region’s crucial agriculture sector. The 6th district is home to nearly 8,000 farms, making it a leader in Virginia’s agriculture industry. The district accounts for over 40% of Virginia’s total agricultural output, and is the undisputed center of Virginia’s livestock industry, producing 89% of the Commonwealth’s meat, eggs, and other livestock-related products. As such, it’s no surprise that Trump’s decision to triple beef imports from Argentina is going to hurt our farmers. The President has gotten a lot of news coverage for his announcement that he’ll pay up to $12 billion to farmers, and while that number sounds impressive it likely won’t do much good for struggling 6th district farms. To start, the majority of the money (as much as $11 billion) will only be available to crop farmers, a massive blow to livestock farmers who are struggling due to Trump’s Argentina beef deal. To make things worse, farmers would have to apply for money, stacking bureaucracy and burden onto a workforce already facing shortages. And, to cap it all off, by the time the money comes–if it comes at all– it’ll be too late for our farmers who typically have to make next season’s crop decisions and place seed, fertilizer, and equipment orders before the end of the year. Meanwhile, and despite representing Virginia’s champion agriculture district, Ben Cline hasn’t said a word about the Argentina beef deal or this new burden on farmers. I share all of this to make the point that 6th district farmers need a champion who will stand up for them, and that’s just what I plan to do in Congress.
2. Republicans’ attacks on immigrant communities. I was outraged last week when Donald Trump called Somali refugees “garbage” and when Republicans (including Ben Cline) refused to denounce this hateful, dangerous rhetoric. More than 5% of 6th district residents are from outside the United States, and almost 10% speak a language other than English at home. In all of this, I’ve often thought of the poem at the base of the Statue of Liberty: “Give me your tired, your poor, your huddled masses yearning to breathe free.” In the Army, I served alongside good men and women from all backgrounds. For many, their parents had been those same tired, poor, and yearning people Lazarus immortalized. For all, the promise of America and the ideals of freedom, security, and prosperity were fundamental to their service. Immigrants in today’s America deserve the same opportunity to serve and contribute to society without prejudice and without oppression. They haven’t given up on that dream. Neither should we.
3. Republicans’ decision to make college degrees more expensive. I’m sure many of you have heard–or read– about the Department of Education’s proposal to remove a wide range of college degrees, including nursing degrees, from its approved list of “professional” degrees. The immediate implications of this change are clear: for students pursuing careers in nursing, counseling, education, accounting, and more, university is about to get a lot more expensive, even unaffordable. But there’s a more troubling aspect to this change that I haven’t been able to ignore: almost all of the fields downgraded from “professional” to “standard” are women-dominated. From an administration that has consistently worked to limit the rights and opportunities of women, this is a blatant attack on good-paying, woman-dominated careers. The good news is that this rule isn’t final: the Department of Education will open a public comment period for input before this policy goes into effect. That public comment period will be our chance to push back against this harmful decision and send a clear message that all education pathways should be valued and protected.

As the holiday season is in full swing, I’ve been hard at work connecting with folks about the need for principled, compassionate leadership in Congress. Time and time again, I hear similar stories about how higher prices are hitting people’s wallets, how congestion not only on I-81 but roads across the 6th district never seems to let up, and how our small businesses are struggling. Behind each of those stories are faces and names. If you have one of those stories: I hear you. I see you. And I’d appreciate the chance to talk with you about how we can make things better. Send me an email at info@kenmitchellforvirginia.com.Until next time, happy holidays.**Ken**
---

name: Voter Action Project
subject: The Supreme Court bailing out MAGA?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Axios - Scoop: GOP expects SCOTUS boost [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121225_1&amount=25]
Straight from Axios , Team: " President Trump's political team privately told big donors that the Supreme Court will soon issue rulings on voting rights and contribution limits that could tilt future elections their way. "
Two main cases are making the MAGA movement feel like things are going to break their way ahead of the 2026 elections:
➜ In Louisiana v. Callais , the court is set to decide whether to gut Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) and return to a time of racial gerrymandering. This would be the capstone of Chief Justice John Roberts career, as he's been working to neuter the VRA since he worked for Ronald Reagan.
➜ In National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) v. the Federal Elections Commission (FEC) , the court is tackling a case that could eliminate a federal law capping how much big-money party committees can spend in direct coordination with favored candidates. This could be the most consequential campaign finance case since the Citizens United decision opened the floodgates of corporate spending in our elections.
To be clear, these cases boil down to a simple idea: do voters get to make the decisions, or can politicians choose their voters and pump unlimited cash into their campaign coffers?
Team: We have to spread the word about these cases and the consequences we would face in the worst comes to pass. Fuel our work to defend voting rights with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121225_1&amount=25]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121225_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121225_1&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121225_1&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121225_1&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121225_1&amount=250] Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121225_1&amount=35]
The potential consequences of these cases are simple to understand and grave for voter participation. The harder we make it to vote, the more politicians make voter participation feel worthless, the less people actually take part. Needless to say, the less people who participate, the less our democracy and our government looks like the people it's supposed to represent.
Section 2 of the VRA requires that racial minorities have an equal opportunity to meaningfully participate in the electoral process. Gutting Section 2 would take us backwards in time and allow racial gerrymandering to be a standard practice, silencing more voters.
In the NRSC case, there are limits on how much political parties and outside entities can coordinate financially with certain candidates. Removing these FEC limits would make it easier for the uber-rich to direct spending and influence elections , limiting the value of voters and making it harder for challengers to enter the political process.
Just like the redistricting fights across the country, this is all about whether the party in power can choose their voters in order to stay in power in perpetuity.
We simply can't afford to go backwards like this. Will you chip in with $25 or more to help us fight back against these partisan efforts to silence voters and protect our fundamental right to vote? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121225_1&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121225_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121225_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax-deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States

name: Tim Walz
subject: Lindell and Trump are coming for Minnesota
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251212-275&t=1&akid=275%2E34582%2EXT3Cfl)
Peter,
**Mike Lindell announced yesterday he’s officially running for Governor of Minnesota.**
That’s right, Donald Trump’s biggest ally in Minnesota is officially in the race.
We know who Mike Lindell is.
❌ He’s a snake oil salesman
❌ He’s an election denier
❌ He’s a con man
If you read that and think it sounds familiar... yes, Mike Lindell and Donald Trump are two peas in a pod.
**Mike Lindell’s got the backing of his MAGA buddies, and we can be sure they’ll empty their pockets to support his campaign. That’s why we’ve launched our 48-hour goal to stop him and his ultrawealthy friends.**
My team has told me we’ve made some great progress, but we’ve still got $25,000 to go.
**[Will you chip in $5 and make sure we’re able to hit our emergency 48-hour goal of $50,000?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251212.2tw2JQ&amount=5&refcode=em20251212-275&t=2&akid=275%2E34582%2EXT3Cfl)**

Tim Walz

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: ICE is terrorizing Minneapolis
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Over the last week we have seen ICE ramp up its racial profiling and violent attacks on Minneapolis residents.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25-ice.Qa0xTJ&refcode=em251212-1-180&t=2&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
Friend — A 20 year-old US citizen and constituent of mine in Minneapolis was dragged out of a restaurant, put in a chokehold, arrested, and detained for hours before being released.
What was his crime? **Looking Somali.**
Over the last week we have seen ICE ramp up its racial profiling and violent attacks on Minneapolis residents.
In another incident, a US-born citizen and Somali-American woman was running errands when she was randomly jumped by ICE agents and zip-tied. They mocked her for her religious attire, touched her inappropriately, and jailed her for over 24 hours. **_A US citizen, 24 hours in jail.**_
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4741?t=3&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
Even those who have turned out to observe ICE activity in their communities have been attacked:
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4742?t=4&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4743?t=5&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
Let me be absolutely clear: **ICE is a rogue gang of thugs, used by the Trump administration to enact violence against their political enemies and enforce Stephen Miller’s white supremacist immigration agenda, civil rights be damned.**
Just click on one of the videos above. Look at how they go out of their way to hurt people who are exercising their Constitutional right to monitor law enforcement and film their activities.
And there is positively no doubt that Minneapolis is being targeted by ICE because Donald Trump is currently obsessed with peddling bigoted rhetoric and conspiracies about Somali-Americans.
Friend, I said during Trump’s first term that **ICE should be abolished.** The Democratic establishment’s failure to embrace that simple idea only enabled the second Trump administration to carry out the violence we are seeing in Minneapolis and across the country.
It is now past time for Democrats to stand together and say that **this must end.** ICE must be abolished if we are to ensure that no President can use immigration enforcement as a secret police force.
[**If you agree that ICE must be abolished and justice must be served to Donald Trump and MAGA officials who allow civil rights violations and attacks on innocent people, join our campaign today with a contribution. We are building a progressive movement that will organize and fight back every day until we win.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25-ice.Qa0xTJ&refcode=em251212-1-180&t=6&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
**[CHIP IN HERE](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25-ice.Qa0xTJ&refcode=em251212-1-180&t=7&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)**
Abolish ICE.
In solidarity,
Ilhan Omar

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=8&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=9&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=10&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=11&refcode2=1997_791427_5C29qm&akid=1997%2E791427%2E5C29qm)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. # [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240529%2E444211%2E37N7eC)
The Supreme Court recently handed down a devastating decision: Texas must use Trump and Greg Abbott’s gerrymandered maps—maps designed to steal five Democratic House seats. This isn’t just politics as usual. This is an attempt to **rig our elections, silence communities, and cement power for the few at the expense of the many.**
The implications are enormous. Competitive districts that should reflect the will of the people are being drawn to favor one party. Entire communities could see their voices diminished. And progress on health care, education, and voting rights could be blocked for years.
**But maps don’t vote. People vote. And we’re not sitting back. Powered by People is doubling down. We’re on the ground in every part of Texas, registering voters, recruiting and training volunteers, and organizing communities to turn out in numbers that no rigged map can suppress.**
It’s going to take hard work and persistence—but it can be done. Texans have overcome unfair systems before, and we will do it again with so much on the line.
[**Will you chip in $3 today to help us fight back against these rigged maps, empower voters, and build a winning infrastructure across Texas? Together, we can make sure every voice is heard and every vote counts.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251212-1.WQBC67&refcode=ema20251212-240529&t=3&akid=240529%2E444211%2E37N7eC)

name: Mark Kelly
subject: President Trump called for me to be hanged…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | President Trump called for me to be hanged.

I put out a video, alongside veterans of the military and former members of the intelligence community. It had a simple message: If you are serving in the military, you have an obligation to refuse illegal orders.

Trump's friends in the White House didn't like that. They accused us of insurrection.

At some point, the President heard about it, and he accused us of sedition, called for our arrest, and called for us to be hanged.

It is no small thing for the President of the United States to call for your execution. Elon Musk called me a traitor. Trump's advisor, Roger Stone, called for me to be executed. And now Trump himself has called for me to be hanged.

As someone whose family has faced political violence before, I know you have to take words like this seriously, and that's why I'm writing to you.

There's no telling what kind of storm we're heading into right now. Unfortunately, we need to be prepared for any outcome and show we won't be deterred by the threats coming our way from Trump and his Administration as we fight to take back the Senate.

It had a simple message: If you are serving in the military, you have an obligation to refuse illegal orders. Trump's friends in the White House didn't like that. They accused us of insurrection. At some point, the President heard about it, and he accused us of sedition, called for our arrest, and called for us to be hanged. It is no small thing for the President of the United States to call for your execution. Elon Musk called me a traitor. Trump's advisor, Roger Stone, called for me to be executed. And now Trump himself has called for me to be hanged. As someone whose family has faced political violence before, I know you have to take words like this seriously, and that's why I'm writing to you. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: He just tapped his credit card at the gas station terminal...| | | | December 12 | | | | | Camera Catches: Best Credit Card Move | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Atlantic Ave., Suite 200 Delray Beach, FL 33444 Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. | | Peter,

I’ll be upfront with you: **Our district is one of the most competitive seats in 2026.**

Keeping this seat blue is key to securing a House majority that will actually put people above partisan football and gamemanship.

But, if my opponent can grab back our district, then outside special interests and GOP Party bosses will control the Congressional agenda – and we just can’t afford that.

**Peter, that’s why it’s essential we keep our fundraising on track. We can’t give my opponent any opening or advantage.**

[But right now, we’re falling behind where we need to be ahead of our final 2025 FEC deadline at the end of the month. They’re revving up to spend millions more and do anything it takes to hand this seat to a yes-man who will do their bidding. I’m fighting back with everything I have, Peter. None of us can sit on the sidelines now. The stakes for our district, state, and nation are too high. = Thank you for standing with me, Marie | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. As the representative for Washington’s third Congressional District, she's working to bring the voices of working people back to DC. Marie works for Washington! = Email is an important way to stay in contact but if you feel like you’re receiving too much, to only receive the most important messages. ** Paid for by MGP Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Marie for Congress, Washington State Democratic Central Committee, and Timber PAC. subject: Getting Congress back to work
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121146537/588509726/148511826?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTU1NTcyMS03Y2Q3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P3hRpKMALWs9IH_NkqgSgo5aVfDgJfjNAaNBLRF1OJ0=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121146539/588509728/-1158826126?refcode=em-12.12.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTU1NTcyMS03Y2Q3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P3hRpKMALWs9IH_NkqgSgo5aVfDgJfjNAaNBLRF1OJ0=)

Hey Peter - it’s Adam Gray, and I’m proud to represent California’s 13th Congressional District in Congress. In 2024, we flipped this seat by just 187 votes. It was the closest race in the entire country.

That margin tells you everything you need to know about how competitive this district is.

We can’t take anything for granted heading into 2026. National Republicans have already made CA-13 a top target—and they’re pouring resources into flipping it back.

**[That’s why I’m reaching out today: Can you split a donation today between our campaign and George Whitesides?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121146542/588509730/-1158826126?refcode=em-12.12.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTU1NTcyMS03Y2Q3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P3hRpKMALWs9IH_NkqgSgo5aVfDgJfjNAaNBLRF1OJ0=)**
| ****[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121146546/588509732/-1158826126?refcode=em-12.12.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTU1NTcyMS03Y2Q3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P3hRpKMALWs9IH_NkqgSgo5aVfDgJfjNAaNBLRF1OJ0=)****
---

I came to Washington ready to work with anyone, no matter what side of the aisle they sit on, to get the job done for the people we were elected to serve. But the current Republican majority in Congress has only brought chaos and dysfunction.

They’ve cut healthcare, slashed food programs for children and seniors, and made it clear their loyalty lies with Donald Trump, not the American people.

My race here in California is a must-win if we’re going to take back the House to get Congress back to work for the American people, and your support can help power us forward.

**[Grassroots supporters like you made the ultimate difference in our race last time around, and we need that same support if we’re going to keep these districts blue and flip the House majority. If you could split a donation between my campaign and George Whitesides, it would go a long way.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121146548/588509734/-1158826126?refcode=em-12.12.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTU1NTcyMS03Y2Q3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P3hRpKMALWs9IH_NkqgSgo5aVfDgJfjNAaNBLRF1OJ0=)**

clean_body: | = = Hey Peter - it’s Adam Gray, and I’m proud to represent California’s 13th Congressional District in Congress. In 2024, we flipped this seat by just 187 votes. It was the closest race in the entire country. That margin tells you everything you need to know about how competitive this district is. We can’t take anything for granted heading into 2026. National Republicans have already made CA-13 a top target—and they’re pouring resources into flipping it back. = | **= I came to Washington ready to work with anyone, no matter what side of the aisle they sit on, to get the job done for the people we were elected to serve. But the current Republican majority in Congress has only brought chaos and dysfunction. They’ve cut healthcare, slashed food programs for children and seniors, and made it clear their loyalty lies with Donald Trump, not the American people. My race here in California is a must-win if we’re going to take back the House to get Congress back to work for the American people, and your support can help power us forward. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thanks for the support, Adam Gray | = = = George has spent his career solving problems. name: Dexter HQ
subject: We need more physicians in the House
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Peter,
Last month , the Senate passed a deal to reopen the government without extending the enhanced premium tax credits for the Affordable Care Act, or proposing anything to curb rising healthcare costs.
Now , we’re a month deep into open enrollment for the ACA—and families are facing doubled, tripled, and sometimes, quadrupled healthcare premiums.
By 2034 , because of the Big, Ugly Bill and if the ACA tax credits aren’t extended, experts predict that around 14 million more Americans could go without health insurance.
At moments like this, it’s painfully clear why we need more physicians serving in government—leaders who actually understand what these policy decisions mean for real patients and their families.
Dr. Maxine Dexter spent her career caring for people in crisis, and now she’s fighting in Congress to protect their access to care. To keep her voice at the forefront of this fight, please consider making a contribution today. Your support helps keep a physician’s perspective at the decision-making table where it belongs. name: Americans for Responsible Innovation
subject: Just Announced: AI Law Preemption EO Virtual Panel
body:
Experts will share insights on the EO’s legal grounding, potential challenges the EO may face in court, and its impact on state laws.

| | | | | |
---
|
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=b90ccf0265&e=a07d75591f)
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | **Just Announced: AI Law Preemption EO Virtual Panel**
Yesterday, President Trump signed an executive order (EO) with the intent of preempting state AI safeguards. On Monday December 15, join a [virtual panel](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=0c773052eb&e=a07d75591f) bringing together legal and policy experts for insights on the EO’s legal grounding, potential challenges the EO may face in court, and its impact on state laws across the country.
The new EO establishes an AI Litigation Task Force charged with bringing lawsuits to challenge state AI laws and tasks the Department of Commerce with cutting Broadband Equity Access and Deployment (BEAD) funding for states that have passed AI safeguards. The EO follows [two failed attempts](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=cd1e203a15&e=a07d75591f) in Congress to pass federal AI law preemption in legislation.

---
| [Register for the Virtual Panel](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=ac5637ab66&e=a07d75591f)
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=89b92858b3&e=a07d75591f)
| | **The AI Law Preemption EO** _Virtual Panel_ Time: 1:30PM ETDate: Monday, December 15, 2025[RSVP here](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=9c84ce32e9&e=a07d75591f)

The upcoming panel features:
* _**Moderator:**_**Doug Calidas,** Senior Vice President of Government Affairs, Americans for Responsible Innovation
* **Charlie Bullock,** Senior Research Fellow, The Institute for Law & AI
* **Slade Bond, Chair,** Public Policy and Legislative Affairs, Cuneo Gilbert & LaDuca, LLP
* **Jamie Susskind** , Partner, ACG Advocacy

The virtual event will be open to the press and the public.

clean_body: Experts will share insights on the EO’s legal grounding, potential challenges the EO may face in court, and its impact on state laws. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Just Announced: AI Law Preemption EO Virtual Panel Yesterday, President Trump signed an executive order (EO) with the intent of preempting state AI safeguards. On Monday December 15, join a bringing together legal and policy experts for insights on the EO’s legal grounding, potential challenges the EO may face in court, and its impact on state laws across the country. The new EO establishes an AI Litigation Task Force charged with bringing lawsuits to challenge state AI laws and tasks the Department of Commerce with cutting Broadband Equity Access and Deployment (BEAD) funding for states that have passed AI safeguards. The EO follows in Congress to pass federal AI law preemption in legislation. | | | The AI Law Preemption EO Virtual Panel Time: 1:30PM ETDate: Monday, December 15, 2025 The upcoming panel features: Moderator:Doug Calidas, Senior Vice President of Government Affairs, Americans for Responsible Innovation Charlie Bullock, Senior Research Fellow, The Institute for Law & AI Slade Bond, Chair, Public Policy and Legislative Affairs, Cuneo Gilbert & LaDuca, LLP Jamie Susskind , Partner, ACG Advocacy The virtual event will be open to the press and the public. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 American for Responsible Innovation. name: George Latimer
subject: The House majority is on the line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by George Latimer for Congress
body:
We have a lot of work to do

George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=3xjxK8AWI%2B%2FnbPg5%2F6xhBlGHI%2BSKSL3SB%2F1p8YpDWSr1tGORsU11TWw0qB%2BwjdzDJ4IXyk%2BmSP0vB5m5ALn%2F8voInuGQYwTFUdm%2BNRbxPTtaBoie%2F3PZnxW9gbYa7ewR%2FrUpnKIfrqPdoBOJmx5TWaB3CrP0l9EU6xMp929rdpuZ3ejTAhvtN5BD6UBD%2B17m%2BPRpAY%2F6RMnYzbUz0854oT7W8hwpjQO9qbO8cEZlkQS17YxdFtG47vk%2FK55ghmC%2BoZgZXbAnKVmmw6QXNi%2BZ4dMtyupXtBuDHcvuGt2SJzBdLmiQ9kzSvtBymNVQrDZC%2FBfCguQ07NxmcDaKCVeQMN%2B5XZerbxBlVsumdPhsvFnev1An8%2FNKDh%2BtbTt6yISEaJBNoe4aIWT%2BkutV%2Fqbau8piIzmVFopTJGihtWcRC7L8oqivSTNiqGyGntr%2FAAwc)
---
| |
---
Peter, We’re entering an election year in just a few weeks — and I want to be clear with you: the work to take back the House majority starts now. Not tomorrow. Not next month. Now. We need to do all we can to take back the House in 2026. **Because the chaos, confusion, and harm we’re seeing from the Republican majority are direct consequences of falling short in 2024.** Republicans went all-in on the Trump agenda to slash healthcare for millions of Americans, SNAP benefits, disaster relief, environmental protections, and more to fund a massive giveaway to the wealthiest individuals in America. **But they’re not stopping now — and we can’t either.** **[That’s why I’m asking grassroots supporters like you to pitch in $10, or anything you can afford so we can reach our $5,000 mid-month deadline.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121149193/588518600/-2078637570?amount=10&source=SBD_GL_EM_FR_121225_MM01_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDJmYzdiMzItN2ZkNy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YgWD-ReLJFBd7poYtVjUCpfu6Db2t51tSvzw2HE-pko=)** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121149197/588518601/-2078637570?amount=10&source=SBD_GL_EM_FR_121225_MM01_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDJmYzdiMzItN2ZkNy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YgWD-ReLJFBd7poYtVjUCpfu6Db2t51tSvzw2HE-pko=)

We must take lessons from the 2024 losses, build on our successes, and most importantly, double down on our efforts to secure even more wins in 2026. Starting right now.
At the end of this month, our fundraising numbers will go public for everyone to see, so we need to meet each and every deadline along the way if we want a shot at taking back the House next November.
**[If you’re with me, chip in $10 to help us reach our mid-month goal and show Republicans that New Yorkers have had enough of their extremist agenda.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121149201/588518603/-2078637570?amount=10&source=SBD_GL_EM_FR_121225_MM01_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDJmYzdiMzItN2ZkNy1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YgWD-ReLJFBd7poYtVjUCpfu6Db2t51tSvzw2HE-pko=)**

clean_body: We have a lot of work to do George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, We’re entering an election year in just a few weeks — and I want to be clear with you: the work to take back the House majority starts now. Not tomorrow. Not next month. Now. We need to do all we can to take back the House in 2026. Because the chaos, confusion, and harm we’re seeing from the Republican majority are direct consequences of falling short in 2024. Republicans went all-in on the Trump agenda to slash healthcare for millions of Americans, SNAP benefits, disaster relief, environmental protections, and more to fund a massive giveaway to the wealthiest individuals in America. But they’re not stopping now — and we can’t either. = | = We must take lessons from the 2024 losses, build on our successes, and most importantly, double down on our efforts to secure even more wins in 2026. Starting right now. At the end of this month, our fundraising numbers will go public for everyone to see, so we need to meet each and every deadline along the way if we want a shot at taking back the House next November. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, George Latimer * | | ---|--- = For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality. | = = Paid for by George Latimer for Congress George Latimer for Congress PO Box 789 White Plains, NY 10602 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: You can also =.

name: Madeleine Foote, LCV
subject: TAKE ACTION: Tell the EPA to protect our waters!
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTMzMDA5NzQS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjM2MDE1OTE4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
Trump’s EPA wants to weaken the rule that delivers clean air for parks, which
will make our air dirtier and put the health and enjoyment of park visitors at
risk. Sign your name and tell the EPA to keep our national parks safe and beautiful and our air clean. https://links.messages.lcv. Peter, It's the EPA’s job to ensure that we have good air quality, including in our
national parks. To protect the health of people who visit our parks and ensure
that they can enjoy incredible places like Yosemite, Glacier, and Acadia
National Parks, Congress required the EPA to use the Clean Air Act to deliver
cleaner air for parks, through what is known as the Regional Haze Rule. Last month, the Trump EPA announced it would continue its pro-polluter agenda by
reconsidering this important rule. Weakening what could be called the “Clean Air
for Parks Rule” will lead to dirtier air and lower visibility in national parks
from pollution that drifts in from dirty coal power plants and industrial
facilities. Big polluters will be off the hook for haze pollution, and our
national parks and public health will suffer. We need to step up and make sure this doesn’t happen. Sign your name and tell Trump’s EPA: Keep clean air in our parks. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/Pwb7C7Zm_ The Clean Air for Parks Rule has already delivered significant benefits since
its implementation 15 years ago, including reducing 1.4 million tons of
visibility-impairing and asthma and heart-attack triggering pollution and over
171 million tons of climate pollution. This rule works, and weakening it will make air quality worse while giving corporations and
polluters another corrupt handout at our expense. We can’t allow the EPA to weaken this time-tested and highly effective rule that
allows federal and state agencies, land managers, and stakeholders to work
together to clean up air pollution so we can breathe clean air and enjoy our
national parks and wilderness areas. Join us in demanding that the EPA stop the rollback of the Clean Air for Parks Rule. Everyone deserves access to clean air, and it’s our responsibility to keep our national parks and wilderness areas safe, visible and accessible for all. https://links.messages.lcv. Thanks for being with us, Peter. Matthew Davis
Vice President of Federal Policy
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=13300974&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM2MDE1OTE4NQS2&spReportId=MjM2MDE1OTE4NQS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=13300974&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM2MDE1OTE4NQS2&spReportId=MjM2MDE1OTE4NQS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/pNy7f3g4E~
clean_body: To view this mail in a browser, copy into your browser. Trump’s EPA wants to weaken the rule that delivers clean air for parks, which will make our air dirtier and put the health and enjoyment of park visitors at risk. Sign your name and tell the EPA to keep our national parks safe and beautiful and our air clean. Peter, It's the EPA’s job to ensure that we have good air quality, including in our national parks. To protect the health of people who visit our parks and ensure that they can enjoy incredible places like Yosemite, Glacier, and Acadia National Parks, Congress required the EPA to use the Clean Air Act to deliver cleaner air for parks, through what is known as the Regional Haze Rule. Last month, the Trump EPA announced it would continue its pro-polluter agenda by reconsidering this important rule. Weakening what could be called the “Clean Air for Parks Rule” will lead to dirtier air and lower visibility in national parks from pollution that drifts in from dirty coal power plants and industrial facilities. Big polluters will be off the hook for haze pollution, and our national parks and public health will suffer. We need to step up and make sure this doesn’t happen. Sign your name and tell Trump’s EPA: Keep clean air in our parks. The Clean Air for Parks Rule has already delivered significant benefits since its implementation 15 years ago, including reducing 1.4 million tons of visibility-impairing and asthma and heart-attack triggering pollution and over 171 million tons of climate pollution. This rule works, and weakening it will make air quality worse while giving corporations and polluters another corrupt handout at our expense. We can’t allow the EPA to weaken this time-tested and highly effective rule that allows federal and state agencies, land managers, and stakeholders to work together to clean up air pollution so we can breathe clean air and enjoy our national parks and wilderness areas. Join us in demanding that the EPA stop the rollback of the Clean Air for Parks Rule. Everyone deserves access to clean air, and it’s our responsibility to keep our national parks and wilderness areas safe, visible and accessible for all. Thanks for being with us, Peter. Matthew Davis Vice President of Federal Policy League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
